title: AtlasStudio Work Order Routing Decision
description: A single routing decision produced by the Work Order Router (studio/orchestration/work-order-router.md) for one work order request. Records classification, target repository, and safety state. This is an audit record, not a dispatch instruction: producing one does not itself create, move, or write any file in any repository.

schema_version.description: Version of this schema the record was produced against.

work_order_id.description: The work order or work package identifier this routing decision applies to.

title.description: Short title of the request being routed, for human readability.

classification.description: Which of the five classification categories the router assigned, per the Classification Model in work-order-router.md.

target_repository.description: The repository the router determined owns this work. 'none' is only valid when routing_status is blocked_ambiguous.

target_path_hint.description: A non-binding hint at where the work would land in the target repository, per the Target Repository / Path Mapping table. The router does not choose a sibling repository's internal paths - this field is advisory only for AtlasStudio-owned paths.

routing_status.description: routed: classification resolved and, if AtlasStudio-owned, safe to file. pending_approval: game_implementation awaiting explicit human approval before any direct write to TheLastSwordProtocol-Game. blocked_ambiguous: classification could not be resolved; no repository assigned. rejected_authority_violation: request attempted to route canon or game_implementation content directly into AtlasStudio and was refused.

requires_explicit_approval.description: True only when classification is game_implementation and the request proposes a direct write to TheLastSwordProtocol-Game rather than an AtlasStudio-side implementation contract.

approved_by.description: Who granted explicit approval, when requires_explicit_approval is true and routing_status has moved past pending_approval. Human identity only, not an agent name.

signals_matched.description: Which rows of the Signals Used For Classification table matched, so the decision can be audited against work-order-router.md.

rationale.description: Human-readable explanation of why this classification and target were chosen, or why the request is blocked_ambiguous.

canon_leak_risk.description: True if the request showed any canon signal but a non-Atlas target was considered before being corrected or rejected.

cross_repo_write_attempted.description: True if the request, as written, asked the router or an agent to write directly into a sibling repository outside the approved game_implementation path.

created.description: Date this routing record was produced.

decided_by.description: Who or what produced this decision, e.g. tools/atlas_router/router.py or a human override, per the Human Correction Logging step in work-order-router.md.
